data.title: Clearmind Medicine registers 17M shares for resale linked to $10M convertible note deal
data.description:
• Clearmind Medicine (NASDAQ:CMND) filed a prospectus to register the resale of up to 17.02M common shares by selling shareholders.
  • This includes: 16.97M shares tied to conversion of $2.5M in initial promissory notes (plus $894,048 in accrued interest).
  • The company entered into Securities Purchase Agreements on September 17, 2025, with institutional investors, issuing $10M in convertible promissory notes for a $9M total purchase price.
  • First initial closing: $555,556 in notes sold for $500,000.
  • Second initial closing (upon SEC effectiveness): $1.94M in notes to be sold for $1.75M.
  • Clearmind may request additional note purchases up to $7.5M (for $6.75M in proceeds) in three-month intervals after the second closing.
  • Source: SEC filing

data.title: Cocrystal Pharma files to sell 5.74M shares of common stock for holders
data.description:
• Cocrystal Pharma (NASDAQ:COCP) has filed to sell 5,736,773 shares of common stock for holders.
  • This prospectus is not an offer to sell these securities.
  • Filing

data.title: Southside Bank CEO Lee Gibson to retire; Keith Donahoe named successor
data.description:
• Southside Bancshares (NYSE:SBSI) on Friday announced the upcoming retirement of Lee Gibson, chief executive officer (CEO) and director of the company and the Bank, effective December 31, 2025. 
  • Gibson will continue as a director of the company and the Bank.
  • The firm has appointed Keith Donahoe, current president of the company and the bank, as CEO, who has over 30 years of banking experience.
  • Donahoe has served as President of Southside since May 2024. Upon joining Southside Bank in 2021, he first served as Austin Market President and later as Central Texas Regional President. Prior to joining Southside, he was with Frost Bank, a regional bank in Texas for 26 years, of which he served the last 10 years as an Executive Vice President.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Visa authorizes $500M deposit into U.S. litigation escrow account
data.description:
Visa (NYSE:V) has authorized the deposit of $500M into the U.S. litigation escrow account previously created under the payment network's U.S. retrospective responsibility plan.

Under the plan's terms, when Visa (NYSE:V) funds the account, the value of the company's B-1 and B-2 common stock, which are held mostly by financial institutions, are subject to dilution through downward adjustments to the conversion rates of the class B-1 to class A common stock and class B-2 to class A common stock.

The deposit and the adjustments to the class B-1 and B-2 conversion rates will be conducted in accordance with the company’s certificate of incorporation currently in effect, it said in a Securities and Exchange Commission filing.

V shares slipped 0.1% in after-hours trading.

data.title: DoubleDown Interactive registers 4.2M ADSs for resale by selling shareholder
data.description:
• DoubleDown Interactive (NASDAQ:DDI) filed a prospectus for the resale of up to 4.21M American Depositary Shares by STIC Special Situation Diamond Limited.
  • The ADSs represent up to 210,632.75 common shares, with each ADS equivalent to 0.05 of a common share.
  • DoubleDown is not selling any shares in this offering and will not receive proceeds from the sale.
  • Source: SEC filing

data.title: 1911 Gold announces upsize of “best efforts” life offering and private placement to C$17M
data.description:
• 1911 Gold (TSXV:AUMB:CA) on Friday said it has entered into an amended and restated agreement with Haywood Securities to amend the terms of its “best efforts” private placement and LIFE offering to increase the size of the offering to C$17 million.
  • Under the terms of the upsized offering, the agents have agreed to sell, on a "best efforts" private placement basis, up to 3.18M common shares which qualify as “flow-through shares” at a price of C$0.64 per CEE LIFE share and 14.8M common shares which qualify as “flow-through shares” at a price of C$0.554 per CDE Offered Share for aggregate gross proceeds to the company from the sale of CEE LIFE Shares and CDE Offered Shares of up to C$10.23M.
  • Additionally, the agents have agreed to sell, on a “best-efforts” private placement basis, up to 6.8M common shares at a price per Non-FT Share of C$0.45 and 5.65M common shares that qualify as “flow-through shares” at the CEE issue price for aggregate gross proceeds to the company from the sale of the Non-FT Shares and CEE PP shares of up to C$6,72M.
  • The marketed offering combines aggregate gross proceeds to the company of up to C$16.95M.
  • The net proceeds from the sale of the Non-FT Shares shall be used for general corporate and working capital purposes.
  • The upsized offering is expected to close on or about Oct 15.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Four Corners Property Trust buys Left Lane Auto property in Mississippi for $3 Million
data.description:
• Four Corners Property Trust (NYSE:FCPT) said on Friday it acquired a newly constructed auto service property leased to Left Lane Auto in Mississippi for $3 million.

  • The property, located in a strong retail corridor, is under a long-term triple net lease with about 15 years remaining.
  • Left Lane Auto operates 67 auto repair shops across 13 states.

  • The deal was priced at a 7.2% capitalization rate on rent, excluding transaction costs.

  • FCPT -0.03% after hours to $25.29.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Snap closes in red after six straight sessions of gains post insider transaction filings
data.description:
Snap (NYSE:SNAP) stock closed 3.32% lower at $8.16 on Friday, September 19, after six straight sessions of gains.

The Snapchat-parent had disclosed four insider sell transactions yesterday post market close. The transactions include CFO Andersen Derek selling 33,871 class A shares in multiple transactions at a weighted average price per share of $7.56.

Chief Accounting Officer Rebecca Morrow sold class A shares worth $18.90K, while Chief Business Officer Ajit Mohan sold shares worth $208.54K.

Snap faces an inquiry from the U.S. Federal Trade Commission concerning the potentially negative impacts of AI chatbots on children and teens.

The FTC had issued orders to seven companies, including Snap, seeking information on how these firms measure, test, and monitor the potential negative impacts.

Nonetheless, the stock had been on a winning streak. The Santa Monica, CA-based technology company added 18.33% over the course of the last six trading days, with a 3.40% gain logged on the day of the FTC probe announcement.

Another notable rise was recorded on Thursday, September 18. The stock closed the day 5.63% higher at $8.44.

SNAP has lost ~24% year-to-date.

Seeking Alpha authors grade the stock as Buy. However, the sell-side analysts as well as the Quant Rating system see SNAP as Hold.

data.title: U.S. drilling rig count gains for third straight week, Baker Hughes says
data.description:
The number of oil and gas drilling rigs in the U.S. gained 3 to 542, Baker Hughes reported Friday in its latest weekly count, rising for the third week in a row for the first time since February.

Oil rigs rose by 2 to 418 during the week, the highest since July, while gas rigs remained flat at 118 and 6 rigs were classified as miscellaneous; the total rig count was down 46 rigs, or 7.8% compared to the same time last year.

Rigs targeting crude oil in the biggest U.S. basins all were unchanged in the past week, with the Permian Basin at 252, the Eagle Ford at 33, and the Williston Basin at 29.

In the DJ-Niobrara shale in Colorado, Wyoming, Nebraska and Kansas, drillers added 2 rigs this week, boosting the count to 11, the highest since April 2024.

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:USO), (UCO), (SCO), (USL), (DBO), (DRIP), (GUSH), (USOI), (UNG), (BOIL), (KOLD), (UNL), (FCG)
